title: Spontaneous Protests Erupt in Southern Cities
detail: Protests break out in Ahvaz, Bandar Abbas, and Bushehr as fuel price hikes take effect alongside food shortages. Demonstrators chant economic slogans targeting government mismanagement rather than explicitly anti-regime rhetoric. Videos shared on social media show crowds of several hundred in each city. Security forces deploy but adopt a watch-and-wait posture.

media.caption: Spontaneous protests erupt in southern Iranian cities over fuel price hikes and food shortages

title: Rial Plunges Past 1.44 Million Per Dollar
detail: The rial crashes through the 1,440,000 per USD barrier on the open market as panic selling accelerates. The Central Bank's intervention of $200 million in forex reserves fails to stabilize the currency. Economists note the psychological damage of repeated record lows is fueling a self-reinforcing flight from the rial, with households converting savings to gold, dollars, and even cryptocurrency.

media.caption: Iranian rial plunges past 1.44 million per dollar as panic selling accelerates

title: IRGC Holds Emergency Security Meeting
detail: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps convenes an emergency security assessment as protests spread to a fourth southern city. IRGC Commander-in-Chief Hossein Salami reportedly orders Basij mobilization contingency plans. State media downplays the protests as 'limited economic grievances being exploited by foreign agents,' signaling the regime's framing ahead of a potential crackdown.

media.caption: IRGC holds emergency security meeting as protests spread across southern cities
